title: The Physics Behind the Poo Emoji’s Shape
author: Jake Currie
text: Subscribe to skip adsAdvertisementPhysicsThe Physics Behind the Poo Emoji’s ShapeAnd how lugworms tweak the rules by pooping upside downJCBy Jake Currie4:03 PM CDT on June 23, 2026Share on FacebookShare on X (formerly Twitter)Share on RedditShare on EmailShare on Bluesky1CommentsEveryone poops, but not everyone poops in the same direction. Take lugworms, for example. These beach-dwelling annelids live in U-shaped burrows dug with their head in one tunnel and their tail in the other. When nature calls, the lugworm excretes a thick stream of wet, sandy excrement upward, right on to the beach (“no dumping” signs be damned). Now this humble worm’s gravity-fighting method of doing its business is shedding light on the physics of feces in a new study published in Nature Communications.Subscribe to skip adsFeatured VideoPowered By 1073.4K 10Lightning Can Actually Spread Across Continents!ShareNextStayWATCH YOUR STEP: This photo of a beach in Northern Ireland shows many casts from lugworms. Credit: Nick Veitch / Wikimedia Commons.“Anyone who has observed animal excreta has likely wondered about the characteristic coiled morphologies that appear across diverse species,” reads the first line of the study. While “anyone” is probably painting with too broad a brush, it was a particular fascination of a certain someone: Charles Darwin. In his later years, the 19th-century scientist became spellbound by earthworms and marveled at their spiral-shaped castings.Subscribe to skip adsAdvertisement
